description_long: This helicopter features a streamlined fuselage with a prominent central body and extended tail boom, designed for efficient aerodynamics. The main rotor is mounted atop the fuselage, with long, slender blades extending outward, while a tail rotor is visible at the end of the tail boom for stability and control. The landing skids are robust and extend laterally from the underside, providing stable ground support. The cockpit area is defined by a rounded, slightly bulbous front section, likely housing multiple windows for pilot visibility. The exterior appears to be constructed primarily from metal, with some composite materials and glass for the windows. The overall color scheme is a muted gray, with subtle variations in shading across different components.
description: A streamlined gray helicopter with a central fuselage, long main and tail rotors, and sturdy landing skids. The cockpit is rounded and likely features multiple windows for visibility.
description_view_0: Front view showing the narrow profile, main rotor blades extending horizontally, and the rounded cockpit section.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the elongated fuselage, tail boom, and the circular main rotor hub, with landing skids visible below.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail boom, tail rotor, and the symmetrical extension of the main rotor blades.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the main rotor, tail boom, and landing skids from the other side.
attribute_rotor_blades: Four long, narrow main rotor blades, likely made of composite material, colored gray with no visible markings.
attribute_tail_rotor: Tail rotor is present at the end of the tail boom, appears to have two blades oriented vertically, colored gray.
attribute_landing_skids: Two parallel landing skids made of metal, colored gray, with crossbars for structural support and stability.
attribute_cockpit_windows: Cockpit windows are not clearly visible, but the rounded front section suggests multiple windows arranged in a wraparound configuration.
